title: Charred, Flaky Uruguayan Pasta Frittata
description: A twist on traditional frittata, featuring charred pasta, frozen mixed berries, and a dash of Uruguayan flair.
instructions: Cook the pasta according to the package instructions, then drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Add the garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the mixed frozen berries and cook for 2-3 minutes, until they start to thaw.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, cheese, salt, and black pepper.
Add the cooked pasta, mixed berries, and chopped parsley to the bowl and stir until combined.
Pour the egg mixture into the skillet and cook over medium heat until the edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15-20 minutes, until the frittata is set and golden brown.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Use a spatula to loosen the edges of the frittata from the skillet, then slide it onto a plate and serve.
